text.title: cloud-run-autoscaling Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cloud-run-autoscaling as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Loading ML models, database connection pools, or large config files inside the request handler on every call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Request-time initialization kills throughput. Load heavy resources once at container startup (or lazily on first request), then reuse across requests for the lifetime of the instance.

failure-mode.description: Running a Pub/Sub message consumer or cron job inside Cloud Run with request-based billing and concurrency=1.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: CPU throttling between requests pauses the message consumer goroutine, causing message lag and invisible failures. Use instance-based billing or switch to a Pub/Sub push subscription that delivers messages as HTTP requests.
